features.summary: Microsoft reported strong Q1 FY25 results with revenue of $65.6 billion (up 16%) and EPS of $3.30 (up 10%). Microsoft Cloud revenue reached $38.9 billion, growing 22%, with Azure growing 33-34% in constant currency. The company's AI business is on track to surpass $10 billion annual revenue run rate in Q2, making it the fastest business in Microsoft's history to reach this milestone. Commercial bookings increased 30% with strong demand for long-term commitments. Microsoft 365 Copilot adoption accelerated with nearly 70% of Fortune 500 companies now using it, and usage more than doubled quarter-over-quarter.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong growth driven by AI and cloud demand. For Q2, they guide Productivity and Business Processes revenue to grow 10-11% and Intelligent Cloud to grow 18-20%. Azure is expected to grow 31-32% in Q2 with stable consumption trends, though some capacity constraints persist. The company expects Azure growth to accelerate in H2 as capital investments increase available AI capacity. Microsoft Cloud gross margin is expected to be around 70%, down year-over-year due to AI infrastructure scaling. Capital expenditures will continue to increase sequentially based on cloud and AI demand signals.
features.risks: The primary risk is capacity constraints limiting Azure growth, with some third-party supply deliveries pushed from Q2 to H2. Microsoft faces external constraints including data center construction timelines and power availability for AI infrastructure. The company is investing heavily in training infrastructure and OpenAI partnership, with $13 billion invested and ongoing equity method losses impacting other income. There's execution risk in managing the rapid scaling of AI infrastructure while maintaining margins and meeting growing customer demand across multiple product lines.
features.key_drivers: AI services contributed approximately 12 points to Azure growth, similar to last quarter, with demand continuing to exceed available capacity. Microsoft 365 Copilot is driving ARPU growth alongside E5 adoption, with enterprise customers returning to purchase additional seats. The Activision Blizzard integration contributed 3 points to company revenue growth. Strong commercial bookings growth of 30% reflects increased long-term customer commitments to the Microsoft Cloud platform. LinkedIn, Dynamics 365, and search advertising all showed healthy growth, with search ex-TAC revenue growing faster than the overall market.
